rulings.Case Title: PAUL A. BATSFORD VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPT, MARINE DIVISION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 24, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion to Quash Service of Summons and Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LIZA IVINS VS. ENOVITY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion Contesting X-Deft'S Application For Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 24, 2010, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT TOOLWORKS, INC. Motion Contesting Cross-Defendants Application for Good Faith Settlement. Motion contesting the good faith settlement is denied. Settlor has provided substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of its liability. Plaintiff fell because she tripped on a mat, and after she fell, the door continued to push her. There is no showing, however, that the door contributed to her injuries, that the door malfunctioned, that Enovity was responsible for any sort of malfunction, or that Enovity in its maintenance of the door or otherwise, was negligent. Enovity has presented evidence showing that the settlement was in the ballpark and Toolworks fails to meet its burden under CCP section 877.6(d). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 24, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, LOUIS WILLACY, DEVIN DWORAK DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is sustained with 10 days leave to amend: As to 2nd cause of action plaintiffs must allege they are consumers. As to the 4th cause of action civil fines are not available. As to the 8th cause of action, plaintiffs must allege that they invested time, skill or money in developing the property. As to the 11th cause of action, plaintiffs other than Briskman and Rosenband must allege a benefit conferred to defendants. As to the individual defendants, plaintiffs must plead facts regarding knowledge or authorization of any illegal acts. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 10th cause of action for declaratory relief as unnecessary in view of prayer for injunctive relief. Demurrer overruled as to all other causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JACOB BRISKMAN et al VS. TAGGED, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 24, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, LOUIS WILLACY, DEVIN DWORAK MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted in part and denied in part. Granted with 10 days leave to amend as to allegations of "conspiracy" (paragraph 73) for plaintiffs to plead facts showing the individual defendants took illegal actions outside the scope of their employment in an effort for personal gain. Granted without leave to amend as to claim to recover criminal fines under P.C. sec. 502 (paragraph 334); claim for civil penalties under B&P Code section 17500 (Prayer for relief C(1), D(1), and paragraphs 346 and 349); claim for punitive damages prayer for relief D(2)); claim for disgorgement of profits for alleged unjust enrichment (however Prayer for relief K(1) must be amended to request appropriate relief); and Briskman and Rosenband's claims for injunctive relief pursuant to Penal Code sec. 502 and the Uniform Trade Secrets Act because they are barred by res judicata. Motion denied as to the other 39 plaintiffs as to injunctive relief pursuant to P.C. sec. 502 and the UTSA. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TAMERA BRIONES VS. RBR DEVELOPMENT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 24, 2010, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED BUILDING SUPPLY, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to 3rd and 4th causes of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 1st cause of action for cross-complainant to plead that cross-defendant owed a duty to cross-complainant or in some way that cross-defendant's actions contributed to cross-complainant's liability to plaintiff. Demurrer sustained with leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action for cross-complainant to plead whether the contract was written, oral, or implied by conduct. Cross-defendant's objections to evidence sustained. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH LEVA et al VS. K2 PARTNERING SOLUTIONS WEST, INC.,A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 24, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT EOIN O'TOOLE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is overruled, 10 days leave to answer. 3 Witken, Summary of California Law, Agency, sec. 199 p. 252 provides that "an agent or employee is always liable for his or her own torts, whether the principal is liable or not, in spite of the fact that the agent acts in accordance with the principal's directions.... Similarly, an agent who commits an independent tort, such as fraud, remains liable despite the fact that the principal, by ratification, also becomes liable." The employee is liable, but may obtain indemnification under Labor Code section 2802 and principles of agency/respondeat superior. The plain reading of Labor Code sections 970 and 972 indicates that O'Toole can be liable for misrepresentations and no clear case authority refutes this. =(302/CWW)
